corrections.text: The article attributes three Wayland-related specifics to Phoronix that the cited Phoronix article does not actually state: that the per-screen virtual desktop feature 'does not function on X11 or XWayland'; that 'KWin now supports the Wayland session management protocol, which lets applications remember their sizes and positions after a restart'; and that multi-GPU handling improves so 'a GPU lacking OpenGL acceleration no longer prevents other GPUs from providing 3D acceleration.' The cited Phoronix piece supports the general 'Wayland-only' direction but does not contain these three specific claims; readers should treat them as uncorroborated by the cited source.

Several direct quotes and feature details are cited to the official KDE announcement at kde.org, which is not on The Machine Herald's source allowlist. kde.org is the official primary source for this release; every load-bearing fact cited to it (the per-screen virtual desktops, Oxygen revival, and HDR+ICC quotes) is independently corroborated by the allowlisted Linuxiac report. The off-allowlist primary is disclosed here for full provenance.
